title: One AI teaches math to check itself while another writes a 50-year proof in an hour - TCR 07/12/2026
description:
OpenAI says GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra proved the 1973 Cycle Double Cover Conjecture using a system allowed to run up to 64 parallel subagents in under an hour, the Bank of England and FCA gain direct oversight of AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft and Oracle from Monday, and BYD won an 11.275 GWh contract for Masdar's Abu Dhabi plant, the largest 24-hour clean-power facility ever contracted.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-july-12-2026/

0:00 Cold Open
0:41 Britain brings the cloud under public oversight
4:31 Britain's Oversight Reaches Inside the Cloud
6:38 Clean energy helps parts of the grid through the heat wave
8:45 BYD wins the world's largest clean-power storage deal
11:49 GPT-5.6 writes a proof to a 50-year graph conjecture

This episode covers the GPT-5.6 graph-theory proof claim and the London Fermat's Last Theorem formalization, Britain designating four cloud providers as critical financial infrastructure, SK Hynix's forecast of a memory shortage lasting beyond 2030, BYD's world-largest solar-plus-storage award, and how clean energy helped carry parts of the US grid through last week's heat wave without emergency curtailment.

The day's signal is capability sprinting ahead while the substrate beneath it gets counted and rebuilt. OpenAI published a proof and prompt for the Cycle Double Cover Conjecture that it says GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra generated in under an hour, using a system allowed to orchestrate up to 64 subagents with adversarial threads assigned to break each candidate step. It remains a lab claim until mathematicians finish checking it, and formalization work in London, where researchers doubled a machine-checkable Fermat proof to 40,000 lines of Lean, is building exactly the verification layer that makes such claims checkable by anyone with the appropriate software and computing resources. Underneath, SK Hynix forecast 2027 as the worst memory shortage on record with demand outstripping supply beyond 2030, and Britain moved the UK cloud arms of Amazon, Google, Microsoft and Oracle under public reliability oversight. Against that scarcity, BYD won the storage contract for Masdar's Abu Dhabi plant, engineered to dispatch a continuous gigawatt of clean power with a bid pricing 24-hour clean power below the alternatives, and in several regions newly added wind, solar, hydropower and discharging EVs helped carry record heat-wave demand with the emergency data-center curtailment authority left unused.
